rationale.R1.2: The section draws a strong causal conclusion ('confirms that RegMagD substantially reduces bias') from aggregate metrics (accuracy, F1, AUC) that do not directly measure magnitude-dependent bias; checking whether the inferential chain from reported numbers to stated conclusion is valid is central to any section review.
rationale.R1.3: This is a results section whose core function is evidential; the headline '~35% reduction in magnitude-dependent bias' is not supported by any bias-specific metric in the artifact—only by global accuracy/F1/AUC deltas—making evidence-claim alignment load-bearing and the most critical review responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: A generic 'review this section' instruction standardly includes prose quality; the section is readable but contains hedging inconsistencies ('substantially reduces' alongside 'approximately 35%') and repetitive sentence structure that warrant polish-level attention.
